Name: Broken
Description: Adds broken pattern to the variety.

Name: Vienna Marked
Description: Adds white spots and/or blaze pattern and/or blue eyes (Vienna Marked).

Name: Vienna Carrier
Description: Carries the Vienna gene (v) but does not show white markings; visually identical to a non-carrier but can produce BEW or VM offspring.

Name: High Roufus
Description: Maximizes the expression of the intermediate band (Wide Band), often referred to as High Roufus in some contexts.

Name: Steeled
Description: Extends Eumelanin expression to the maximum; often results in a darker appearance with ticking.

Name: Booted
Description: A broken pattern showing too much color, resulting in minimal white pattern usually mostly on the underside and at the feet.

Name: Blanketed
Description: A broken pattern that is more than the typical broken pattern, but not as extreme as booted.

Name: Harlequinized
Description: Applies the harlequin pattern (patchy distribution of Eumalanin and Pheomelanin).

Name: Martenized
Description: Applies the Marten pattern (Dark Chinchilla) as an override to the color locus.

Name: Self Chin
Description: Applies both the Self (aa) and Dark Chinchilla (cchd) patterns as an override.

Name: Non-Vienna
Description: Denotes that the rabbit is clean from Vienna lines.

Name: Single Mane
Description: Produces a single mane (Mm).  Results in a weakened appearance compared to a double mane.

Name: Double Mane
Description: Produces a double mane (MM).  Typically required for true Lionhead expression.

Name: Supersteel
Description: Homozygous Steel; extends Eumelanin expression to the absolute maximum, typically resulting in a solid color appearance in Agouti rabbits.

Name: Gold Tipped
Description: Specifies Gold Tipped expression, typically used with Steel varieties.

Name: Silver Tipped
Description: Specifies Silver Tipped expression (Dark Chinchilla), typically used with Steel varieties.

Name: Tri
Description: Produces a tri-color pattern (Harlequin + Broken).

Name: Chin
Description: Suffix modifier for Chinchilla pattern (Dark Chinchilla).

Name: True Charlie
Description: Genetically homozygous broken (EnEn), often resulting in very little color.

Name: False Charlie
Description: A rabbit that resembles a Charlie but is genetically heterozygous broken (Enen).

Name: Moon Eye
Description: A genetic defect that causes blindness, usually by 6 months of age.

Name: Moon Eye Carrier
Description: Carries the moon eye gene (me) but has normal vision.
